input_dict.agent_role: Integrate second-hop evidence with first-hop context and retain the facts needed to answer the question.
input_dict.question: Carlo Emilio Gadda and Katharine Kerr both were considered what?
input_dict.current_output: Both Carlo Emilio Gadda and Katharine Kerr were novelists. The passages corroborate that Gadda was an Italian writer and author of novels (see passages 1 and 3), while the context identifies Kerr as an American science fiction and fantasy novelist.

upstream_context.question: Carlo Emilio Gadda and Katharine Kerr both were considered what?
upstream_context.summary_1: Evidence: Carlo Emilio Gadda is described as "an Italian writer and poet" and is the author of novels (e.g., "That Awful Mess on Via Merulana is an Italian novel by Carlo Emilio Gadda"); Katharine Kerr is described as "an American science fiction and fantasy novelist." Bridge/missing fact: both share the role "novelist" (or more broadly "writer"); next hop (if needed) — retrieve a source explicitly calling Gadda a "novelist" to confirm the exact shared label.
hop2_titles: That Awful Mess on Via Merulana
hop2_docs: Carlo Emilio Gadda | Carlo Emilio Gadda (] ; November 14, 1893 – May 21, 1973) was an Italian writer and poet.  He belongs to the tradition of the language innovators, writers that played with the somewhat stiff standard pre-war Italian language, and added elements of dialects, technical jargon and wordplay.
Eros e Priapo | Eros e Priapo: da furore a cenere is a 1945 satiric pamphlet by Italian author Carlo Emilio Gadda.

That Awful Mess on Via Merulana | That Awful Mess on Via Merulana is an Italian novel by Carlo Emilio Gadda, first published in Italy by Garzanti Editore s.p.a. in 1957.  An English translation by William Weaver was published in 1965.

Un maledetto imbroglio | Un maledetto imbroglio also known as "The Facts of Murder" is a 1959 Italian crime film mystery directed by and starring Pietro Germi with Claudia Cardinale.  The film is based on the novel by Carlo Emilio Gadda "Quer pasticciaccio brutto de via Merulana".  The film was restored and re-released in 1999 both in Italy and the USA.
Piero Gadda Conti | Piero Gadda Conti (13 February 1902 – 22 January 1999) was an Italian novelist and film critic.
Carlos Emilio Morales | Carlos Emilio Morales (November 6, 1939 – November 12, 2014) was a Cuban jazz guitarist.

Carlo Emilio Bonferroni | Carlo Emilio Bonferroni (28 January 1892 – 18 August 1960) was an Italian mathematician who worked on probability theory.  Carlo Emilio Bonferroni was born in Bergamo on 28 January 1892 and died on 18 August 1960 in Florence.  He studied in Turin, held a post as assistant professor at the Turin Polytechnic, and in 1923 took up the chair of financial mathematics at the Economics Institute in Bari.  In 1933 he transferred to Florence where he held his chair until his death.
